pattern: Because all words share one length, a fixed-width sliding window over each starting offset can count word frequencies and match the required multiset exactly.
hints: Every concatenation has the same total length: number of words times word length, and each window splits cleanly into equal-size chunks.
A start index works only if the chunks at that position form exactly the multiset of words, counting repeats.
Slide a window of length len(words)*wordLen; at each start, cut it into wordLen pieces and compare their frequency map to the words' frequency map, recording the start on a match.

edge_inputs.description: two distinct words, two valid starts
repeated word must appear the right number of times
words present but never adjacent in any order

stress.description: string built from a small word set so concatenations occur, plus noise
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    wlen = rng.randint(1, 3)
    nwords = rng.randint(1, 4)
    alphabet = 'abc'
    pool = [''.join(rng.choice(alphabet) for _ in range(wlen)) for _ in range(nwords)]
    words = [rng.choice(pool) for _ in range(nwords)]
    parts = []
    target_len = max(wlen, size)
    while sum(len(p) for p in parts) < target_len:
        if rng.random() < 0.5:
            order = words[:]
            rng.shuffle(order)
            parts.append(''.join(order))
        else:
            parts.append(''.join(rng.choice(alphabet) for _ in range(rng.randint(1, wlen))))
    s = ''.join(parts)
    return [s, words]
stress.note: word length kept tiny so the window scan stays fast; planted concatenations create matches

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def findSubstring(self, s, words):
        from collections import Counter
        wlen = len(words[0])
        k = len(words)
        total = wlen * k
        need = Counter(words)
        n = len(s)
        res = []
        for offset in range(wlen):
            left = offset
            count = 0
            window = Counter()
            j = offset
            while j + wlen <= n:
                w = s[j:j + wlen]
                j += wlen
                if w in need:
                    window[w] += 1
                    count += 1
                    while window[w] > need[w]:
                        lw = s[left:left + wlen]
                        window[lw] -= 1
                        left += wlen
                        count -= 1
                    if count == k:
                        res.append(left)
                        lw = s[left:left + wlen]
                        window[lw] -= 1
                        left += wlen
                        count -= 1
                else:
                    window.clear()
                    count = 0
                    left = j
        return res
